programmes.show_description: A Cry for Justice: Nathaniel Parker stars in the gripping murder mystery series from the mind of Elizabeth George. A murder in Oxford takes Lynley away from home and his wife.
A Present from Dublin: Christopher Timothy talks to the animals once more. Calum welcomes the new arrivals - one that bites and one that fits Siegfried's domestic requirements.
Cuckoo: Comedy from darkest Essex - Chigwell to be precise. Sharon gets a shock when she tries to collect her unemployment giro.
Murder in the Dark: Part One: When a man is killed at her university reunion, Becks finds herself on the wrong side of an investigation. Was a friend responsible?
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Schooling: More laughs with/at the Chigwell duo. Sharon joins the upper crust and gets taken for a ride when she is wooed by the charming Mark.
Judgement Day: Lennox and Rawton are drawn closer together when a dangerous ghost from Rawton's past returns to haunt her.
New. Sweet Success: Nick helps a boy claiming to have an allergy to chocolate. At the end of her tether with public transport, Vivien declares she is going to take up driving again.
New. The Ticking Clock: A woman discovers she is pregnant by her younger lover and the blood test reveals she is HIV positive. Meanwhile, Vivien ignores Jimmi's advice at an auction.
Letting Go: Connie feels Will's bedside manner needs improving, but as she digs into his past she uncovers a few secrets. Diane returns to work for the first time since her sister's death.
Truth Will Out: Jay starts a petition against an administrative adviser, while Ruth tries to persuade Edward they should come clean about their sham marriage.
Judgement Day: Lennox and Rawton are drawn closer together when a dangerous ghost from Rawton's past returns to haunt her.
New. As events in the Vic reach their shocking climax, Whitney realises that Billie Jackson is in way over his head. Meanwhile, Jack and Chelsea get spicy in the Argee Bhajee.
New. Whitney gives Billie an ultimatum, while Carol fears for her son's future. Janine and Ryan's new money-making scheme almost shows their soft side.
The Salt of the Earth: Hankies at the ready in a moving episode of the vets drama. Siegfried is saddened by the death of a young bull terrier - one of an inseparable pair of dogs.
Ken and Orrin Plot against Charles while he is busy voicing his concerns over Laura and Gerald's relationship.
Too Much of Water: It's not all plain sailing when Frank and Lu are hired to look into the drowning of a former Miss Stratford-upon-Avon.
You Pays Yer Money: Sharon isn't registered to vote in Chigwell, but when election fever hits the constituency, she wants to make use of Darryl's vote.
Goodnight Sweet Ferret: Gentle humour and the odd bit of slapstick in Roy Clarke's popular sitcom. Hobbo, Alvin and Entwistle visit Old Heptonstall, who's confined to his shed.
Behind Every Bush There Is Not Necessarily a Howard: Charming sitcom, here in its 31st series! Hobbo enlists the services of his 'team' to help Toby win back the love of his ex-wife.
Happy Camping: More laughs with those amiable oldies. When Pearl throws Howard out of the house, he goes to Clegg for sympathy. Meanwhile, Barry embarks on a new fitness regime.
New. Smoke & Mirrors: Final Chapter: Despite Mattie's objections, Beth and Cat continue to pursue leads on their own, while the theatre company is forced to change the cast around.
The Tree of Truth: A body is identified as a woman who went missing seven years earlier - raising the possibility that an innocent man confessed. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams.
God's Waiting Room: Amanda Redman stars in the light-hearted crime drama about the charismatic ageing coppers. The team look into a suspicious death in an old people's home.
If Wishes Were Horses: Another absorbing case for the sleuths from two ends of the social spectrum. As Lynley looks forward to fatherhood, his wife Helen helps him with a case.
The Salt of the Earth: Hankies at the ready in a moving episode of the vets drama. Siegfried is saddened by the death of a young bull terrier - one of an inseparable pair of dogs.
Schooling: More laughs with/at the Chigwell duo. Sharon joins the upper crust and gets taken for a ride when she is wooed by the charming Mark.
Murder in the Dark: Part Two: As Judith and Suzie work with the police to find the killer, tensions rise among Becks' friends in the house. Guest starring Harry Enfield.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Business: Comic sibling rivalry in Chigwell. The sisters fall out when Sharon takes on Chris's inheritance - a Greek cafe! Tracey is recruited to work as a skivvy.
Haunted: The Sun Hill CID must deal with ghosts, clairvoyants and messages from beyond the grave.
New. What the World Needs Now: Eva clashes with a charismatic preacher about how best to solve street crime and Vivien asks Julia to give her a driving lesson.
New. Holding On: Melody helps a woman come to terms with her daughter's death, and Julia and Vivien have a bad driving lesson.
Deception: Connie tries to seduce Will, but has much more success with Mubbs, while a patient's wife is horrified to discover her husband is having an affair. Terri Dwyer guest stars.
Grandiosity: Ruth undermines Zoe by revealing she's the doctor with the highest mortality rate in the ED. Lenny has a psychiatric evaluation before becoming his sister's donor.
Haunted: The Sun Hill CID must deal with ghosts, clairvoyants and messages from beyond the grave.
New. Billie Jackson's final attempt to turn his back on his past backfires when an act of revenge puts Jack in the firing line. Plus, Ryan has had enough of Janine's scheming ways.
New. A social worker comes to interview Zsa Zsa and Ben - any slip-ups and Louise is back in care. However, it all goes smoothly and Phil's delighted Louise will be home.
Cheques and Balances: Classic TV in every sense with the vets of pre-war Yorkshire. Siegfried is determined to make the farmers pay bills and he assigns James to skinflint Biggins.
Leo worries Abby is having her baby for the wrong reasons, and Ken sets the Fraud Squad on Charles.
Most Wicked Speed: When an American PI is arrested for murder, Frank and Lu set off in search of a valuable muscle car.
Trust: Tracey falls for her smooth therapist Gerald and argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship when he bans her from continuing her sessions.
The Rights of Man (Except for Howard): Another trip to Yorkshire for some senior silliness. Hobbo decides that the still-homeless Howard needs to show Pearl who's boss!
Howard and the Great Outdoors: 31 series, and still with a spring in its step! Hobbo and Co turn Howard into a tramp to show Pearl how rough he's been living. Will she take him back?
Look Whose Wheel's Come Off: The young-at-heart sitcom. Glenda sends Barry off to paint a nice view, to take his mind off food. A dejected Howard wonders if Pearl still loves him.
The Jackdaw's Revenge: The amateur sleuth struggles to protect those closest to him when an old foe is unexpectedly released from prison.
The gripping drama series about a Yorkshire police sergeant continues. Catherine searches for the owner of the suspicious Milton Avenue property, with dramatic consequences.
Ducking and Diving: James Bolam and Dennis Waterman are veteran cops in the popular crime series. A case is re-opened when a security van is found at the bottom of a lake.
